updateModal.step2: 2. Open the file and install Natively.

about.title: About Natively
about.tagline: Designed to be invisible, intelligent, and trusted.
about.modesManagerDescription: Seven specialized AI personas — Interview, Sales, Recruiting, Team Meet, Lecture, Technical, and General. Each mode injects a tailored system prompt, reference files, and smart note sections into every response.
about.customContextDescription: A free-form textarea in Profile Intelligence lets you inject any personal context — sales stats, product notes, LeetCode patterns, preferences — into every AI call as a structured block. Auto-saves as you type.
about.realTimeProDescription: Pro feature gates (Modes, Profile Intelligence) now update instantly when you activate or deactivate a license — no restart needed. Active mode is automatically cleared on license loss.
about.sttStabilityDescription: Deepgram reconnect storms are now prevented via exponential backoff and connection staggering. Server-side key pooling supports up to 6 Deepgram and ElevenLabs keys with automatic rotation on failure.
about.hybridIntelligenceDescription: Seamlessly routes queries between ultra-fast models for instant speed and reasoning models (Gemini, OpenAI, Claude) for complex tasks. Powered by enterprise-grade speech recognition from 7+ providers.
about.localRagDescription: A purely local vector memory system allows Natively to recall details from past meetings. Embeddings and retrieval happen on-device via SQLite for maximum privacy.
about.stealthControlDescription: Features "Undetectable Mode" to hide from the dock and "Masquerading" to disguise as system apps. You control exactly what data leaves your device.
about.noRecordingDescription: Natively listens only when active. It does not record video, take arbitrary screenshots without command, or perform background surveillance.
about.creator: Creator
about.founderBio1: I build software that stays out of the way.
about.founderBio2: is made to feel fast, quiet, and respectful of your privacy.
about.starOnGithubDescription: Love Natively? Support us by starring the repo.
about.reportIssueDescription: Found a bug? Let us know so we can fix it.
about.getInTouchDescription: Open for professional collaborations and job offers.

help.title: Help & Setup Guide
help.subtitle: Learn how to deeply configure Natively. Everything from providing the right API scopes to executing conversational interviews seamlessly is covered below.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.subtitle: Get Natively running in four steps.

permissions.title: Grant Permissions
permissions.desc: Enable Screen Recording and Accessibility for Natively in macOS Privacy & Security.

audio.title: Set Up Audio
audio.desc: Open Settings → Audio and select Natively API, or paste a Deepgram or Google key.

ai.title: Connect an AI Model
ai.desc: Open Settings → AI Providers and choose a built-in model, or add a Groq or OpenRouter key.

mock.transcriptSnippet: ...and I'd also consider a distributed cache layer for horizontal scaling
mock.sampleQuestion: So how would you optimize the current algorithm?
mock.sampleAnswer: A good approach would be to use a hash map to cache intermediate results and reduce time complexity to O(N).
mock.askPrefix: Ask anything on screen or conversation, or
mock.summaryText: Discussed microservice architecture for the new payment gateway. Analyzed Redis vs Memcached for caching with a focus on data persistence to prevent race conditions during checkout.
mock.keyPoint1: Redis chosen for sorted set support enabling O(log N) rate limiting.
mock.keyPoint2: Horizontal scaling via distributed cache layer discussed.
mock.transcriptQ: Why did you use Redis over Memcached for the cart session?
mock.transcriptA: Because we needed sorted sets for rate limiting and automatic expiry without custom cron jobs.
mock.redisQuestion: Could you elaborate on the Redis rate limiting?
mock.redisAnswer: You mentioned Redis Sorted Sets for the sliding rate window — efficient because it auto-expires stale records while keeping operations strictly O(log N).
mock.apiDependenciesQuestion: What API dependencies did they mention?
mock.apiDependenciesAnswerPrefix: Based on the transcript near 10:45 AM, they explicitly mentioned integrating
mock.apiDependenciesAnswerSuffix: to handle the recurring tier logic securely.
mock.permissionsHint: Natively requires Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions to analyze screen context.
mock.logoLauncherHint: — clicking this brings you right back

permissions.title: 1. App Permissions Setup
permissions.intro: Natively operates entirely on-device, but requires OS permissions to tap into your screen context and global keystrokes. Here is how your system should look:
permissions.loopbackDesc: Natively can capture both what you say and what you hear globally. At the top of Audio Settings, use the dropdowns to select your hardware input and output capture. By default, Natively uses System Default, so audio routing follows your OS preferences.
permissions.sckDesc: The recommended backend for macOS 13.0+. Uses Apple's modern, optimized framework for low-latency speaker capture.
permissions.coreAudioDesc: Fallback engine for older hardware. Only use this if SCK repeatedly drops speaker packets.
permissions.languageDescPrefix: Below the provider list, specify the
permissions.languageDescMiddle: you will be speaking. Most importantly, select your regional
permissions.languageDescSuffix: mapping, such as en-US, en-GB, or en-IN, so STT backends can improve transcription accuracy.
permissions.screenRecordingDesc: Provides Natively the ability to read your screen temporarily when you capture context.
permissions.screenRecordingPath: System Settings > Privacy & Security > Screen Recording
permissions.accessibilityDesc: Required for Natively to detect global keyboard shortcuts regardless of focused window.

audio.title: 2. Audio STT Providers Setup (Microphone)
audio.intro: Natively supports over 8 audio engines to transcribe what you hear and say. From the Audio tab in settings, use the dropdown to switch the active engine.
audio.apiKeysDesc: We strongly recommend testing connections before joining a live meeting. The system shows successful pings or explicit errors if credits or permissions fail.
audio.googleSetup: Uses a Service Account JSON instead of an API key. Create a GCP project, activate Cloud Speech API, create a Service Account under IAM, download the JSON key, and drop it into Audio Settings.
audio.elevenlabsSetupPrefix: When creating a Custom API key for the Conversational AI streaming integration, explicitly enable
audio.groqSetupPrefix: Provides ultra-fast Whisper responses via LPUs. Starts with
audio.openaiSetupSuffix: Remember: this audio key is isolated from your AI generation key under AI Providers.
audio.deepgramSetup: Extremely accurate streaming transcription. Key is generated on-demand in the Deepgram Console.
audio.azureSetupSuffix: You must also specify an Azure Region alongside your key, such as eastus or westeurope, or requests will fail.
audio.ibmSetup: Create a Watson Speech to Text resource in IBM Cloud and generate API credentials.
audio.sonioxSetup: Provides high-end medical and legal tier transcription using the standard dashboard API key.

ai.title: 3. AI Providers & Prompt Engine
ai.intro: Natively uses Large Language Models (LLMs) to reason about your screen and audio context. You can configure cloud providers, local models, or fully custom endpoints.
ai.groqDesc: Ultra-fast inference using LPU hardware. Default model:
ai.openaiDescPrefix: Industry standard pipeline. Chat routed via Netmind:
ai.registrySyncPrefix: Natively uses a 14-day background sync clock
ai.registrySyncSuffix: to silently poll upstream APIs. If Anthropic or OpenAI drops a new flagship architecture, your app dynamically absorbs it into the UI dropdown.
ai.activeModelDescPrefix: Inside the Launcher UI, you can hot-swap your
ai.activeModelDescSuffix: This determines the active reasoning engine for screen analysis and responses.
ai.ollamaIntro: You can run Natively completely offline with 100% data privacy using Ollama. Natively automatically scans
ai.ollamaStep2: Open Terminal and run the recommended 8B instruction model:
ai.ollamaStep3: Alternatively, for faster generation without GPU, use Microsoft's smaller model:
ai.ollamaStep4: Return to Natively's AI Providers overlay to see local models ready for use.
ai.customProvidersDesc: Use Custom Providers to hook up any standard external LLM router such as OpenRouter, LMStudio, or proprietary company endpoints. Create a new provider using a cURL command template.
ai.responsePathDesc: Tell Natively how to parse JSON text back. Deeply nested outputs must define the exact path array. For OpenAI/OpenRouter compliant endpoints, use:

interface.title: 4. Natively Interface Operations
interface.intro: When initialized, Natively hides itself visually while remaining active as a persistent translucent overlay. This is your command center.

whatToAnswer.title: What to Answer?
whatToAnswer.desc: Reads the active transcript and screen, then streams a precise response to read aloud.

brainstorm.title: Brainstorm
brainstorm.desc: Recap becomes Brainstorm when Interview Mode is ON — deep multi-step strategies.

followUp.title: Follow Up
followUp.desc: Suggests the next logical question to keep conversation flowing gracefully.

answerNow.title: Answer Now
answerNow.desc: Records your mic + screen context and fires an immediate AI query.

clarify.title: Clarify
clarify.desc: Generates sharp probing questions from latent audio when a topic is unclear.

recap.title: Recap
recap.desc: Condenses the last 5 minutes into bullet points when you lose the thread.

codeHint.title: Code Hint
codeHint.desc: Reads your screen and nudges you toward the correct code implementation.

screenshotAsk.title: Screenshot & Ask
screenshotAsk.desc: Forces a full-screen capture and immediately processes it through the LLM.

stealthExecute.title: Stealth Execute
stealthExecute.desc: Processes context in the background without ever revealing the interface.

meeting.title: 5. Meeting Intelligence
meeting.intro: When an active session concludes, it is saved locally as a complete intelligence dossier spanning transcript, AI token usage, and automated summaries.
meeting.summaryExecutionDesc: Natively runs a local background job after the meeting to compress the raw transcript into clean markdown with overviews and action items.
meeting.rawTranscriptsDesc: Dive into the exact dialogue timeline. Speaker separation classifies Me vs Them and preserves timestamps.
meeting.usageStorageDesc: View how many tokens the AI consumed across the meeting, tracking visual and textual inputs separately.
meeting.semanticSearchDesc: Instead of re-reading the transcript, use the attached RAG interface pinned to the Meeting details window.
meeting.contextualSearchDesc: Ask plain questions and the system injects the relevant localized transcript timeline to answer accurately.
meeting.memoryIsolationDesc: Conversations here are isolated to the selected meeting and do not use global memory.

globalSearch.title: 6. Global Search & Shortcuts
globalSearch.introSuffix: anywhere to invoke the Natively Global Palette, a Spotlight-style overlay for interacting with the system.
globalSearch.instantTraversalDesc: Typing instantly fuzzy matches previous meeting titles, summaries, and internal files. Press enter to jump into that topic.
globalSearch.conversationalFallbackDesc: If your query doesn't match a document, the palette can start a standard LLM chat with your typed intent.
globalSearch.systemShortcutsDescPrefix: These hotkeys work anywhere, regardless of whether Natively is focused or hidden. Change them via
globalSearch.showHideInterfaceDesc: Quickly toggle window visibility. Used as an immediate panic hide.
globalSearch.captureScreenshotDesc: Takes a silent screenshot in the background and feeds visual data to the LLM context flow.
globalSearch.processCapturedDesc: Triggers Natively to analyze captured screenshots and text from the rolling buffer.
globalSearch.captureExecuteDesc: Captures a screenshot and processes it in one action.

pro.title: 7. Pro Intelligence
pro.profileSystemDesc: Profile Intelligence parses your background and injects it into all queries so responses are customized to your role.
pro.zeroContextPrepDesc: Model inherits your coding stack, experience, and other context.
pro.resumeParsingDesc: Upload your PDF resume for local extraction.
pro.proFeature: This is a Natively Pro feature.
pro.dropResume: Drop your Resume PDF in the UI to activate injection.
pro.customContextDescPrefix: In addition to your Resume and JD, type free-form context into the
pro.customContextDescMiddle: textarea — sales stats, product details, LeetCode solutions, or preferences. Injected as a
pro.customContextDescSuffix: block into every AI call, across all modes and providers.
pro.useStep2: Scroll to the Custom Context textarea
pro.useStep4: Up to 4,000 characters with a live counter
pro.putProductDetails: Your company's product details or pricing

modes.title: 8. Modes Manager
modes.intro: Modes assign a specialized AI persona to your session. Each mode has a tailored prompt, personal context, reference files, and note templates.

interview.name: Interview
interview.desc: STAR-format answers, behavioral stories, step-by-step coding hints.

sales.name: Sales
sales.desc: Objection handling, discovery questions, product pitch frameworks.

recruiting.name: Recruiting
recruiting.desc: Candidate assessment, JD cross-referencing, structured evaluation.

teamMeet.name: Team Meet
teamMeet.desc: Action items, announcements, blockers, decisions — auto-extracted.

lecture.name: Lecture
lecture.desc: Concept breakdowns, intuition-first explanations, formula notes.

technical.name: Technical
technical.desc: DSA / system design reasoning, edge cases, complexity analysis.

modes.openStep1: Click the grid icon in the Launcher header
modes.openStep2: Or click the grid icon in the main interface toolbar
modes.activateStep1: Select any mode in the left sidebar
modes.activateStep2: Click Set active to apply it
modes.activateStep3: The toolbar icon shows the active mode name live
modes.activateStep4: Click Deactivate to return to General
modes.referenceStep1: Upload PDF, DOCX, or TXT files per mode
modes.referenceStep2: File contents are injected as real-time context
modes.referenceStep3: Great for: resumes, product sheets, job descriptions
modes.referenceStep4: Per-file cap: 12 k chars · total cap: 40 k chars
modes.customStep1: Click + New Mode to create from scratch
modes.customStep2: Or use Natively Templates for a preset start
modes.customStep3: Write a custom real-time prompt for the AI
modes.customStep4: Add your own note section templates
modes.proFeatureDesc: Modes are locked behind Natively Pro. Free and trial users see only General mode. Activate a license via Settings → Natively API to unlock all seven modes.

misc.title: 9. Miscellaneous
misc.calendarDesc: By connecting Google Calendar, the AI gains context on upcoming meetings, syncs event data, and reads attendee lists to personalize interactions.
misc.calendarStep1: Navigate to the Calendar tab in settings.
misc.calendarStep2: Click Connect Google Calendar and authenticate securely.
misc.followUpDescPrefix: When tracking live meetings, Natively uses connected calendar context to figure out
misc.followUpDescSuffix: This powers Follow-Up Email drafts to confirmed attendees.
misc.fastModeDesc: Fast Mode activates efficient models layered over Groq hardware LPUs instead of GPUs. This can bring latency below 500ms. To enable it, open the Settings popup and click the Lightning icon.

stealth.title: 10. Stealth & Window Control
stealth.disguiseDesc: Natively is designed for minimal operation. It can disguise its process and remain invisible to standard screen-recording and desktop sharing utilities.
stealth.opacityDescPrefix: Decrease your visual footprint. In General Settings, drag the
stealth.opacityDescSuffix: down to make the interface translucent against native applications.
stealth.mousePassthroughDescPrefix: Want the AI prompt fused into your screen without blocking clicks? Activate
stealth.mousePassthroughWarning: This makes the Natively overlay unclickable. Memorize Global Hotkeys, such as Cmd+Shift+Arrows to move, Cmd+B to hide, and Cmd+1-7 for actions, before enabling it.

meetingEnd.title: End meeting?
